stream_samples.text: exported a 300dpi PDF for print and the embedded PNGs got resampled to 150dpi. printer rejected the file. canva pls fix
Magic Resize cropped my client's logo out of the corner on every IG variant. The safe-area logic doesn't respect locked elements anymore.
[#71204 P1] Pro user — PDF Print export downscales raster fills to 150dpi regardless of source asset resolution, started ~April 18
Devanagari text in our Hindi templates is rendering with broken conjuncts in PDF export — looks fine on screen, prints garbled
brand kit primary color shifted from #1A4FFF to #1B50FE on every shared template overnight. nobody on the team made an edit
Video timeline scrubbing on iPad Pro M2 is laggy beyond 30s clips. The playhead jumps in 2-3 frame increments now.
Pro account hit "AI image generation rate limit" after 12 prompts today. Last month I could do 50+ before throttling. Did the SLA change silently?
Print-pipeline regression: ImageMagick worker is normalizing all raster fills to 150dpi before merging into the PDF stream. Bisected to deploy 2026.04.16.
Brand kit color drift complaints up 380% week-over-week — looks like an sRGB->Display P3 round-trip on shared templates
[export-pdf] PDF/X-1a profile dropping ICC color tags on raster layers, downstream prepress flagged as out-of-gamut
magic studio's new background remover is genuinely better than photoshop now, this is wild
Arabic RTL templates: text-on-path is reversing letterforms after the Magic Resize step. Was fine last week, broken since Tuesday.
anyone know how to force canva to keep my CMYK swatches as CMYK on export? it keeps converting to RGB
Magic Resize for Pinterest 1000x1500 crops the bottom CTA out 90% of the time when the original is square. Used to anchor correctly.
Video editor: scrubbing past the 60s mark on M2 iPad Pro causes the timeline to detach from the preview. Have to refresh the doc.

top_epic.title: Restore 300dpi raster fidelity in PDF Print export pipeline
top_epic.rationale: 2,640 signals — heavily weighted toward Pro and Teams customers running print workflows — bisect cleanly to deploy 2026.04.16, when the ImageMagick worker began normalizing raster fills to 150dpi before merging into the PDF/X-1a stream. Prepress shops are rejecting files; brand-kit ICC tags are also being dropped in the same pass.
top_epic.verdict: Revenue-critical — print workflows are the core Pro retention loop.
top_epic.recommendation: Roll back the resampler default, preserve source DPI through the worker chain, and reattach ICC color tags for PDF/X-1a exports.
acceptance_criteria: Raster fills export at source DPI (up to 600dpi) without resampling
ICC color tags survive the PDF/X-1a merge step on every layer
Pre-flight checker warns user if export DPI < 300 for Print presets
P95 PDF export time remains within 10% of pre-regression baseline
